label: 第159話
source_locator: 単行本18巻 目次 p.127
summary_short: 日車寛見が弁護士として不条理な裁判と向き合ってきた過去が描かれる。無罪を信じた被告への有罪判決を機に術式が覚醒し、法廷で木槌を振るう。
summary_full: 国選弁護人の日車寛見は、殺人・強盗の容疑を否認する大江圭太の弁護を担当する。証拠は不利だったが、警察の違法な対応や大江の生活環境を調べ、第一審で無罪を勝ち取る。しかし世論の反発と検察の控訴を受け、控訴審は大江を無期懲役とした。大江の絶望した視線を見た日車は、正しさを求め続けた司法への失望を決定的にし、法廷で術式を覚醒させる。

candidate_evidence.summary_short_legacy: 日車寛見 is a criminal defense lawyer who takes difficult cases because he believes in the truth. He's currently defending young 大江圭太 , who has been unfairly branded guilty by the broken justice system. Pushed to the edge by one unjust verdict after another, the future 死滅回游 player decides to issue judgment of his own.
candidate_evidence.summary_full_legacy: Keita pleads his innocence despite the overwhelming evidence against him. In Japan, ninety-nine percent of criminal trials end in a guilty verdict. 日車寛見, a public defense lawyer for criminals, speaks with his client, 大江圭太. Keita explains that he had a cat who was lost and while looking for it, he was confronted by the police and decided to flee because his cat would've died. It's a rule where he lives that he can't have a cat so no one knows that he has one. In March of 2016, a mother and daughter were both slain in Morioka City in Iwate Prefecture. The prosecutors suspected 大江圭太, who is a local resident. They arrested him and charged him with suspicion of murder and robbery. After Keita fled the initial questioning by police officers, they followed him to his home and found a bloody blade. Lab analysis confirmed the blood on the knife matched the DNA of the victims. Despite the outstanding evidence and practically being caught red-handed by the cops with the murder weapon in his possession Keita insisted that he was innocent. Not even 日車's associate Shimizu believes Keita. 日車 reviews Keita's case with his office co-worker named Shimizu. Keita told 日車 that he picked up the blade even though it wasn't his. Shimizu doesn't believe that and believes he fled from authorities because he was guilty. 日車 argues trauma because Keita was illegally harassed by police officers in the past because of an acquaintance who was using drugs. Keita claims he was going to give the knife to the police later because his live-in job didn't allow him to call the police or ambulances. His job runs as a shelter for the elderly who have nowhere to go but they didn't pay wages. Employees are only given money under the table on the new year on top of lunches and groceries while still being overcharged 50,000 yen a month for rent. More than a few residents have a criminal record and ever since earthquake relief fund money started pouring into the region, more shady nonprofit businesses have begun appearing. 日車 believes it's likely that the weapon was actually lying around and Keita is actually telling the truth. Keita thanks Higiruma after they win an innocent verdict in the first trial. 日車 forms a plan to take on the case. He wants to continue gathering information while Shimizu takes care of all the other logistics. Shimizu thinks she's getting the bulk of the work and is even asked to look after Keita's cat. Shimizu believes that public defenders like them are underpaid and overworked. In a murder case like this one, there's no chance of a suspended sentence and the defendant can even receive the death penalty. Shimizu doesn't understand why they're even trying in such a hopeless case but 日車 states that taking difficult cases once in a while to keep their skills sharp. Annoyed, Shimizu thinks out loud and says that it's not "sometimes". Shimizu meets with a friend and fellow lawyer named Takagi inside the cafe she owns. Takagi used to work with 日車 and apparently he never changes. He's the type who isn't interested in money and Takagi believes he may have even gotten worse. She gives an example of a past case where 日車 defended a nineteen-year-old who was forced to drink and drive by his job. The defendant was angry with 日車 because he wasn't found innocent. 日車 never told him he would be found innocent, only that he could get his sentence suspended. Takagi pointed out the way clients abuse their lawyers and how it isn't worth it but 日車 replied that he understands that people are just against the wall financially and emotionally, so they take it out on their lawyers. Concerned, Takagi asked about how it was affecting 日車's mental state. 日車 is mortified that Keita is unjustly convicted in the second trial. 日車 and Shimizu work tirelessly until the day of the trial and manage to earn an innocent verdict for Keita. Shimizu is surprised they actually got the verdict but 日車 knows the prosecution will immediately appeal. In the media, the court of public opinion assumes that the prosecution was bribed, that the wrong verdict was reached, and justice had failed in some capacity, the opposite of the truth. In a post-trial meeting, 日車 reveals to Keita that nothing from the robbery that was stolen was found in his room and someone disappeared from the residence soon after the crime. Above everything else, Keita also appeared on convenience store camera footage at the same time as the estimated time of death. Keita thanks 日車 but the lawyer simply emphasizes there will be a second trial. Sobbing, Keita specifies that he means he just wants to thank 日車 for believing in him. " Even if I'm the only one... I want to keep my eyes open. " However, at the second trial, Keita is unfortunately found guilty and sentenced to life in prison. Independent defense lawyers like 日車's office rely on limited funding and small staffing. Public prosecutors have access to tax money and much greater manpower. No new evidence was even presented at the second trial and the court passed out this sentence on the shaky argument that there was no reason to suspect the missing person. A third trial before the highest court is unlikely because they refuse to hear most cases. Getting a fair hearing is improbable and the system unfairly branded Keita guilty from the very beginning. Desperate and angry, Keita looks at 日車 with sorrow and disappointment in his eyes. 日車 is shocked and asks himself why the clients look at him in this way, losing touch with the rationalization he gave Takagi. Back when Takagi asked him about his mental state, 日車 replied that it wasn't his intention to save the weak. He's always been the type of person who couldn't ignore things. Justice is supposed to be blind and people close their eyes to spare themselves from harsh truths, but 日車 wants to keep his eyes open even if he's the only one. 死滅回游 Player: 日車寛見. The judge announces that objections to the guilty verdict against 大江圭太 must be filed within fifteen days. 日車 abruptly interrupts the announcement by slamming his table with a judge's gavel of his own. This surprises the prosecutor, the judge as well as Shimizu, who backs away from her partner as a curse begins to form behind him. Refusing to allow the trial to end in this unjust manner, 日車 snaps and tells everyone that nobody is allowed to leave because they're having a retrial. 日車 has been chosen as a 死滅回游 player and this event has served as a catalyst for his powers to awaken.
candidate_evidence.characters_raw_text: 大江圭太 日車寛見 Shimizu Takagi 名称不明 Judge 名称不明 Prosecutor
candidate_evidence.techniques_raw_text: 式神 ジャッジマン

key_events_verified_phase9: 日車が大江圭太の刑事弁護を担当する
第一審で無罪判決を得るが控訴審で無期懲役となる
大江の絶望を前に日車の術式が覚醒する
verification_notes_phase9: 集英社公式18～20巻目次と巻概要、日本語の原作漫画巻別・話別記事、旧候補資料を話単位で突合した第九段階。単行本各ページの直接最終照合前。
